justification: All 5 core rules identified (BR-1 through BR-4 as invariant: true, BR-5/6/7 as invariant: false for weight brackets). State machine directionality explicitly modeled — in_transit->pending forbidden (BR-2, AC-6) and delivered terminal (BR-3, AC-7). 14 acceptance criteria all in Given/When/Then format, far exceeding the minimum 3. Testable assertions are concrete and developer-actionable (e.g., 'weight <= 0 or weight > 1000 must be rejected'). Weight bracket rules correctly marked invariant: false with clear justification. Confidence level 55 is within reasonable range given the agent identified genuine ambiguities, though below the 65-90 band specified in the rubric — the stated reasoning for the lower confidence is defensible given the real gaps identified.

observations.text: Identified 7 rules total, exceeding the 5 expected — broke weight brackets into per-bracket rules with boundary-specific testable assertions
State machine directionality thoroughly covered: backward transitions forbidden, delivered terminal, and even Pending->Delivered skip identified as an edge case
All 14 acceptance criteria use clean Given/When/Then format with concrete preconditions and expected outcomes
Invariant classification is perfect — all state/data integrity rules marked true, all pricing bracket rules marked false with clear justification
Edge cases section identifies boundary precision issues (10 kg, 50 kg) and operational gaps (missing Cancelled/Returned states) — shows deep domain thinking
Escalations are well-structured with options and recommendations, demonstrating the agent's role as domain authority

Confidence level at 55 is below the rubric's 65-90 pass band, though the reasoning is substantive — the agent identified real ambiguities but may be overly conservative given the spec is fairly unambiguous on the core rules

AC-14 (Pending->Delivered skip) assumes a constraint not explicitly stated in the original spec — while defensible, it's presented as definitive rather than flagged as an assumption within the acceptance criteria itself

grader_results.detail: field confidence.level value 55 is below min 65
